commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=031b454269b0a61073dce538b2afca33992a79fd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates plugin drift diagnostics so exact npm-pinned official plugins are shown with package@gateway-version update commands in doctor and gateway status output.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +130. Total +166 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main prints id-only or generic `plugins update` hints for drift, while docs and update selection show explicit npm specs are the supported way to override exact pins. I did not run a live seeded doctor/status command in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to fix the command-target bug and provide real CLI proof; maintainers also need to resolve the overlap with the open bulk-update recovery PR.

target.title: fix(cli): show working commands for pinned plugin drift
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where operators who saw official managed plugin version drift after a beta upgrade could be given a repair command that did not actually advance exact npm pins.

During a Windows upgrade to `2026.6.10-beta.1`, `doctor` / `status --deep` reported active official plugins such as `brave`, `discord`, `qqbot`, and `whatsapp` still on `2026.6.9`. The suggested `openclaw plugins update <plugin-id>` command was not sufficient for those records because their install records were exact npm pins like `@openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.9`; an id-only update preserves that pin and reports the stale version as up to date.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps the existing plugin update semantics intact and fixes the operator guidance instead. Plugin drift now resolves the update command for each drift entry: floating installs still use `openclaw plugins update <plugin-id>`, while exact npm pins use an explicit `openclaw plugins update <package>@<gatewayVersion>` target.

Out of scope: changing manual `plugins update <id>` behavior, unpinning installs automatically, or changing how version drift is detected.

## User Impact

Operators can copy the drift repair command from `openclaw doctor` or `openclaw gateway status --deep` and get the plugin onto the gateway version. For the beta upgrade case above, the advice becomes:

```text
openclaw plugins update @openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.10-beta.1 && openclaw gateway restart
```

instead of an id-only command that can preserve `@openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.9`.

## Evidence

Before evidence from the real upgrade:

```text
backup SQLite install records before manual repair:
brave    spec=@openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.9  resolvedVersion=2026.6.9
discord  spec=@openclaw/discord@2026.6.9       resolvedVersion=2026.6.9
qqbot    spec=@openclaw/qqbot@2026.6.9         resolvedVersion=2026.6.9
whatsapp spec=@openclaw/whatsapp@2026.6.9      resolvedVersion=2026.6.9
codex    spec=@openclaw/codex@2026.6.10-beta.1 resolvedVersion=2026.6.10-beta.1
```

Observed symptom during that upgrade: `openclaw plugins update brave`, `discord`, `qqbot`, and `whatsapp` each reported the `2026.6.9` install as up to date while the gateway was already on `2026.6.10-beta.1`; manually installing `@openclaw/<package>@2026.6.10-beta.1 --force --pin` repaired the drift.

After-fix source smoke for the status formatter:

```text
Plugin version drift: 1 active official plugin not on gateway 2026.6.10-beta.1
- brave: 2026.6.9 (npm) -> expected 2026.6.10-beta.1
Fix: openclaw plugins update @openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.10-beta.1 && openclaw gateway restart.
```

After-fix source smoke for the doctor workspace note:

```text
Plugin version drift
1 active official plugin not on OpenClaw 2026.6.10-beta.1
- brave: 2026.6.9 (npm) -> expected 2026.6.10-beta.1
Fix: openclaw plugins update @openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.10-beta.1 && openclaw gateway restart.
```

Local validation:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-version-drift.test.ts src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.print.test.ts
# 3 shards passed: 20 tests, 17 tests, 11 tests

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src\plugins\plugin-version-drift.ts src\plugins\plugin-version-drift.test.ts src\commands\doctor-workspace-status.ts src\commands\doctor-workspace-status.test.ts src\cli\daemon-cli\status.print.ts src\cli\daemon-cli\status.print.test.ts
# exited 0

node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check --threads=1 src\plugins\plugin-version-drift.ts src\plugins\plugin-version-drift.test.ts src\commands\doctor-workspace-status.ts src\commands\doctor-workspace-status.test.ts src\cli\daemon-cli\status.print.ts src\cli\daemon-cli\status.print.test.ts
# all matched files use the correct format

git diff --check
# passed
```

What was not tested: full CI and packaged npm release artifacts. The change is limited to drift repair text and focused formatter/helper tests.

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
